items.prompt: Plot the point `(3, -2)` and explain what each coordinate controls.
items.expected_evidence: identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the slope between `(1, 4)` and `(5, 12)` and interpret it as a rate of change.
items.expected_evidence: graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Determine whether the table represents a function and justify your decision.
items.expected_evidence: connect periodic graph behavior to angle movement around the unit circle
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates connect periodic graph behavior to angle movement around the unit circle with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain how a table, graph, and equation can represent the same linear relationship.
items.expected_evidence: identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets.

items.prompt: Explain what slope means in both graphical and contextual terms.
items.expected_evidence: graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features.

items.prompt: A student reverses the coordinates and plots `(3, -2)` as `(-2, 3)`. Diagnose the mapping error. Focus on angle coordinate period fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets.

items.prompt: A student says every straight-looking graph is proportional. Identify the misclassification. Focus on unit circle period opacity.
items.expected_evidence: graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features.

items.prompt: A ride-share charges a fixed fee plus a cost per mile. Write a rule, make a table, and interpret the slope and intercept.
items.expected_evidence: identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Given a graph and a verbal description, decide whether they could represent the same relationship and justify the match.
items.expected_evidence: graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves graph and interpret bounded sine, cosine, and tangent behavior using structural features in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, move between a rule, a table, and a graph for a linear relationship and explain the connection.
items.expected_evidence: identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify and explain unit-circle coordinates and corresponding trigonometric values in bounded angle sets on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on right triangle trigonometric ratio foundations to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by trigonometric functions and unit circle itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether right triangle trigonometric ratio foundations is secure enough to support trigonometric functions and unit circle
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in right triangle trigonometric ratio foundations from a current-node issue in trigonometric functions and unit circle.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on coordinate plane and ordered pairs to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by trigonometric functions and unit circle itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether coordinate plane and ordered pairs is secure enough to support trigonometric functions and unit circle
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in coordinate plane and ordered pairs from a current-node issue in trigonometric functions and unit circle.
